title: Research open-source and self-hosted architecture options
scope: Evaluate practical architectures for cross-editor live autocomplete using open-source components, local models, OpenAI-compatible proxies, and Anthropic/OpenAI APIs. Include whether a shared backend can serve both VS Code and Obsidian and what custom plugin work would be required.
goals: Research FIM-capable code/text models and serving stacks relevant to autocomplete, such as Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, Tabby, LiteLLM, OpenRouter, and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
Rate feasibility separately for local-only, API-key, OpenAI-compatible proxy, and unofficial subscription/session-proxy architectures.
Identify which approaches support low-latency streaming or completions suitable for inline suggestions.
Assess feasibility of using GPT/Claude subscriptions versus API keys in each architecture.
Provide an implementation path for a custom Obsidian plugin if no off-the-shelf true autocomplete exists.
non_goals: Do not write the final report.
safety: Do not read tmp/, archive/, or prompt-buffer.md.
Do not edit files tagged human-written.
Use current web sources for project status.
success_criteria: Include architecture patterns, required components, risks, and source URLs.
Clearly rate feasibility for VS Code and Obsidian separately.
